source.title: Coordinated representational reinstatement in the human hippocampus and lateral temporal cortex during episodic memory retrieval

confidence_rationale: Staresina et al. demonstrate a temporal cascade: hippocampal pattern completion (~500ms) precedes cortical reinstatement of item information in lateral temporal cortex. This supports MECH-150's architecture where ContextMemory (hippocampal analogue) produces a cue-indexed context that then influences downstream processing (E2/E3). The temporal ordering establishes the causal direction MECH-150 assumes.
failure_signatures: The temporal cascade (500ms hippocampal -> cortical reinstatement) operates on a timescale far slower than REE's per-step inference. MECH-150 assumes context retrieval is instantaneous within a forward pass.
The study uses item-context associations (object-scene pairs), which is simpler than the multi-dimensional cue context MECH-150 is designed to produce.

mapping.source_claim_statement: During episodic memory retrieval, hippocampal pattern completion of item-context associations occurs at ~500ms post-cue, followed by reinstatement of item information in lateral temporal cortex. This temporal ordering establishes the hippocampus as initiating retrieval, with cortical reinstatement as a downstream consequence.
mapping.ree_translation: MECH-150's ContextMemory produces cue-indexed associations that then feed downstream to E2 (action biasing, MECH-151) and E3 (terrain scaling, MECH-152). The temporal cascade described here supports this causal architecture: context retrieval (ContextMemory) precedes and drives downstream processing (E2/E3 influence), not the reverse.
mapping.mapping_caveat: The biological temporal cascade involves oscillatory dynamics (high gamma associated with pattern completion) and operates over hundreds of milliseconds. REE's ContextMemory is a feedforward computation within a single inference step. The temporal ordering principle maps well; the dynamic mechanism does not.
mapping.source_context: Intracranial recordings in epilepsy patients during episodic memory retrieval, hippocampal and lateral temporal cortex

confidence_components.notes: Nature Communications, intracranial recordings (rare and valuable data). Mapping fidelity moderate: the temporal cascade principle maps well onto MECH-150's architecture but the temporal dynamics themselves do not. Transfer risk moderate: intracranial human data provides excellent biological grounding but REE's implementation is feedforward.
